Aztecs.history: The Aztec Empire dominated Mesoamerica through military conquest and sophisticated urban planning. Even after Spanish conquest, Aztec descendants preserved their cultural identity and military traditions.
Aztecs.playstyle: Aggressive early game with strong Eagle Warriors; focus on building strong defensive perimeter with unique units.
Aztecs.units: War Dog, Eagle Warrior, Jaguar Warrior, Puma Spearman
Aztecs.bonus: Priests can build shrines to generate experience; strong eagle warrior line
Aztecs.ageup: Eagle warrior shipments and native alliance cards

British.history: Queen Elizabeth I's England established naval dominance and expanded colonial holdings globally through merchant ventures and trading posts. Britain pioneered industrial advancement and built a maritime empire.
British.playstyle: Economy-focused with strong navy advantage. Manor-spawned settlers enable aggressive booming. Wall harbor early, then push naval and Musketeer lines.
British.bonus: Manors spawn Settlers automatically; strong shipping and naval economy
British.ageup: Wagon politicians (Trading Post, Bank, Manor shipments)

Chinese.history: The Kangxi Emperor presided over the Qing Dynasty's expansion and prosperity, establishing China as a dominant continental and maritime power. Confucian traditions guided governance and military affairs.
Chinese.playstyle: Defensive, village-based economy with strong gunpowder units. Use multiple town centers and cohesive unit formations for disciplined warfare.
Chinese.units: Qiang Pikeman, Chu Ko Nu, Mongol Horse Archer, Fire Lance Cavalry
Chinese.bonus: Multiple towncenters for economic advantage; monastery generates XP
Chinese.ageup: Imperial age shipments and technology research

Dutch.history: Maurice of Nassau revolutionized military tactics and led the Dutch Revolt against Spanish dominance. Amsterdam became the center of a mercantile empire controlling global trade routes.
Dutch.playstyle: Economic power through trade and banking. Strong cavalry and merchant fleet; focus on sea control and defensive fortifications.
Dutch.bonus: Banks and merchants generate gold passively; strong traders and navy

Ethiopians.history: Menelik II defeated Italian colonial forces at Adwa, securing Ethiopian independence and modernizing the kingdom. Ethiopia maintained its sovereignty while other African nations fell to European colonization.
Ethiopians.playstyle: Strong elite units and defensive play. Limited but high-quality military with focus on well-coordinated formations.
Ethiopians.units: Shotel Warrior, Archers, War Elephant, Mounted Archer
Ethiopians.bonus: Elite warriors with bonus defensive stats; can build unique religious structures
Ethiopians.ageup: War elephant and elite warrior shipments

French.history: Louis XVIII restored the Bourbon monarchy after Napoleon's fall, stabilizing France through cautious diplomacy. France remained a cultural and diplomatic powerhouse despite post-Napoleonic instability.
French.playstyle: Elegant and flexible military with strong cavalry. Effective at both offense and defense through balanced unit composition.
French.bonus: Home City Shipments arrive faster; cavalry units more effective

Germans.history: Frederick the Great transformed Prussia into a military superpower through disciplined tactics and artillery innovation. German military culture emphasized precision, formation discipline, and rapid mobilization.
Germans.playstyle: Mass production of disciplined line infantry and artillery. Effective at breaking enemy lines through concentrated firepower and formation tactics.
Germans.bonus: Mass-produce cheap line infantry; Guard upgrades available earlier

Haudenosaunee.history: Hiawatha unified the Six Nations Confederacy, establishing a sophisticated governance system based on consensus and mutual defense. The Haudenosaunee maintained their independence through strategic alliances and superior tactics.
Haudenosaunee.playstyle: Mobile and aggressive with strong scouting and raiding capabilities. Build through settlements and alliances with other native tribes.
Haudenosaunee.units: Tomahawk, Travois, War Canoe, Iroquois Warrior
Haudenosaunee.bonus: Native allies provide bonuses; settlements and lodges generate experience

Hausa.history: Muhammadu Kanta expanded the Kebbi kingdom through military prowess and established dominance over Hausa lands. Hausa cities became centers of Islamic learning and trans-Saharan trade.
Hausa.playstyle: Cavalry-focused warfare using superior mounted archers and warriors. Control territory through fast-moving mounted units and defensive fortifications.
Hausa.units: Cavalry Archer, War Elephant, Musketeers, Royal Guard
Hausa.bonus: Cavalry units are faster and more effective; trade routes generate gold

Inca.history: Pachacuti transformed the Inca Empire into the largest pre-Columbian state in the Americas through military conquest and administrative innovation. Terrace farming and road networks unified vast mountain territories.
Inca.playstyle: Strong economy through tribute and terraced farming. Build extensively but defend carefully; specialized units for mountain warfare.
Inca.units: Inca Warrior, War Chief, Slinger, Llama Herds
Inca.bonus: Terraces provide food; road network enables faster movement

Indians.history: Shivaji Maharaj founded the Maratha Empire, resisting Mughal dominance and establishing Hindu sovereignty. His guerrilla tactics and naval power challenged the largest empire in India.
Indians.playstyle: Versatile and adaptive military strategy. Strong in both open field and defensive play with effective cavalry and elephant units.
Indians.bonus: Villagers gather resources faster; elephants are strong assault units

Italians.history: Garibaldi unified Italy through military campaigns and popular revolution, ending centuries of fragmentation. Italy emerged as a modern nation-state with growing industrial and naval power.
Italians.playstyle: Balanced military with strong artillery and cavalry. Italian discipline and tactical flexibility enable both aggressive and defensive strategies.
Italians.bonus: Artillery and cavalry receive combat bonuses; defensive structures stronger

Japanese.history: Tokugawa Ieyasu established the Tokugawa Shogunate, bringing peace and stability to feudal Japan. Bushido culture and military discipline defined Japanese society for centuries.
Japanese.playstyle: Disciplined and honorable warfare with strong melee units and archers. Excel at defensive play and controlling terrain through fortifications.
Japanese.units: Samurai, Ashigaru Musketeer, Naginata Rider, War Monk
Japanese.bonus: Samurai and melee units extremely strong; temples generate XP

Lakota.history: Gall led the Lakota people during the era of Great Plains warfare and cultural resistance. The Lakota became renowned for their exceptional horsemanship and warrior traditions.
Lakota.playstyle: Lightning-fast cavalry raids with strong scouting and harassment. Effective at hit-and-run tactics and controlling open plains terrain.
Lakota.units: Mounted Archer, Travois, Dog Soldier, Sioux Commando
Lakota.bonus: Mounted units faster and harder to hit; hunting for extra food

Maltese.history: Jean de Valette defended Malta against Ottoman siege, establishing the island as a Christian stronghold in the Mediterranean. Malta became a center of naval power and religious authority.
Maltese.playstyle: Strong defensive and naval play with excellent fortifications. Control the sea and defend positions; strong mercenary and naval shipments.

Mexicans (Standard).history: Hidalgo launched the Mexican Independence War, sparking revolutionary fervor across Latin America. Mexico emerged as an independent nation with rich cultural traditions and strategic territory.
Mexicans (Standard).playstyle: Flexible and revolutionary with strong priest and mounted units. Haciendas provide economic advantage; cavalry and mercenaries form the backbone.
Mexicans (Standard).bonus: Haciendas generate extra resources; cathedral for unique improvements

Ottomans.history: Suleiman the Magnificent ruled the Ottoman Empire at its apex, dominating three continents through military innovation and administrative genius. Ottoman culture blended Islamic, Persian, and Byzantine influences.
Ottomans.playstyle: Powerful gunpowder-focused military with excellent siege capability. Janissaries and Abus Gunners provide firepower; flexible and adaptable.
Ottomans.bonus: Gunpowder units strong; mosques generate XP and research bonuses

Portuguese.history: Henry the Navigator pioneered global exploration, establishing Portuguese dominance of oceanic trade routes and African commerce. Portugal built the first truly global maritime empire.
Portuguese.playstyle: Naval-focused with strong mercantile advantage and early gunpowder units. Control sea lanes and establish trading posts for economic dominance.
Portuguese.bonus: Naval units strong; trading posts provide economic advantage

Russians.history: Ivan the Terrible expanded Moscow into a vast empire, establishing Russia as a major European power. Russian military strength and vast territory defined the emerging superpower.
Russians.playstyle: Powerful and resilient with strong mounted archers and streltsy. Economic resilience through Cossacks and vast resources; defensive depth.
Russians.units: Streltsy, Cossack, Mounted Archer, Heavy Cannon
Russians.bonus: Siege powerful buildings effectively; cavalry and archers strong

Spanish.history: Isabella of Castile sponsored Columbus's voyage and unified Spain through the Reconquista. Spain became a global superpower through conquest and exploration in the New World.
Spanish.playstyle: Conquistador-focused with strong priests and explorers. Build missions for influence; use crossbowmen and pikemen for disciplined formations.
Spanish.bonus: Missionaries convert natives; conquistadors very strong

Swedes.history: Gustavus Adolphus revolutionized military science and made Sweden a great power through disciplined tactics and innovation. Swedish military dominance shaped 17th-century European conflicts.
Swedes.playstyle: Disciplined and innovative military tactics with mobile artillery and strong cavalry. Effective at breaking lines through concentrated force.
Swedes.bonus: Artillery very mobile and accurate; cavalry discipline and damage high

United States.history: George Washington led the American Revolution against British rule, establishing the United States as an independent nation dedicated to liberty and democratic principles.
United States.playstyle: Militiamen and minutemen provide flexible defense and rapid mobilization. Strong irregular warfare and defensive capabilities with economic resilience.
United States.bonus: Militia rapidly rebuilt; strong defensive economy

Americans.history: Thomas Jefferson expanded America westward and established democratic ideals. Post-revolutionary America pursued expansion, technological innovation, and industrial development.
Americans.playstyle: Industrial and expansionist with strong settlers and economic output. Build settlements and railroads; mechanized army focused on artillery.
Americans.bonus: Settlers fast; industrial bonuses for production

Argentines.history: José de San Martín liberated Argentina and neighboring territories from Spanish colonial rule. Argentina emerged as a prosperous nation with vast agricultural lands and European immigration.
Argentines.playstyle: Agricultural focus with strong cavalry and revolvers. Use open plains for cavalry dominance; economic advantage through estancias.
Argentines.bonus: Cavalry and gauchos very strong; estancia bonuses

Baja Californians.history: Manuel Pineda Muñoz led Baja California's resistance against filibuster invasions and internal disorder. The region struggled for stability while maintaining its distinct identity.
Baja Californians.playstyle: Flexible and adaptable with strong settlers and ranching economy. Use saloons and mercenaries; cavalry-focused military.
Baja Californians.bonus: Saloons enable mercenary training; ranching economy

Barbary.history: Hayreddin Barbarossa and his successors built a pirate empire dominating Mediterranean trade. Barbary States challenged European naval supremacy for centuries.
Barbary.playstyle: Naval corsairs and pirates with strong privateers. Control sea lanes and raid merchant shipping; naval warfare and privateer economy.
Barbary.units: Corsair Marksman, Tribal Horseman, Barbary Warrior, Galley
Barbary.bonus: Privateers raid shipping; naval units very strong

Brazil.history: Pedro I declared Brazilian independence from Portugal and established a stable empire. Brazil developed as the largest and most economically powerful nation in South America.
Brazil.playstyle: Strong economy through plantations and African units. Flexible military with revolutionaries and strong artillery.
Brazil.units: Voluntario da Patria, Lancer, Rodelero, War Dog
Brazil.bonus: Plantations and slavery economy; strong unit multiplier

Californians.history: Mariano Guadalupe Vallejo led California through Mexican independence and American conquest. Californios became legendary horsemen and frontier leaders.
Californians.playstyle: Cavalry-dominated with vaqueros and rancheros. Use open terrain for mounted advantage; saloons and ranching economy.
Californians.bonus: Vaqueros extremely fast; ranching economy strong

Canadians.history: Isaac Brock defended Canada against American invasion, earning military renown. Canada developed as a frontier power balancing British loyalty and continental independence.
Canadians.playstyle: Frontier defense with strong villagers and local alliances. Trappers and voyageurs provide economic advantage; ranged units effective.
Canadians.bonus: Trappers generate furs; frontier defense strong

Central Americans.history: Francisco Morazán championed Central American federation and independence from Spanish colonial rule. The region struggled with fragmentation while maintaining distinct identity.
Central Americans.playstyle: Flexible and adaptive with strong artillery and defense. Use terrain and fortifications effectively.

Chileans.history: Bernardo O'Higgins led Chilean independence and established a stable republic. Chile developed as a disciplined military power with strong institutions.
Chileans.playstyle: Disciplined military with strong cavalry and infantry. Effective at both offensive and defensive play through tactical excellence.
Chileans.bonus: Cavalry discipline exceptional; military training fast

Columbians.history: Simón Bolívar liberated Colombia and surrounding territories, envisioning Gran Colombia. Colombia became the center of Bolívar's revolutionary empire in South America.
Columbians.playstyle: Revolutionary and aggressive with strong mounted units. Use cavalry for rapid strikes and mountain terrain for defense.

Egyptians.history: Muhammad Ali Pasha established Egyptian independence within the Ottoman sphere, modernizing the nation and establishing a dynasty. Egypt became the most powerful Arab state.
Egyptians.playstyle: Strong military through mamluke traditions and modernization. Cavalry and mamelukes form elite striking force.
Egyptians.bonus: Mamelukes very strong; military training enhanced

Finnish.history: Carl Gustaf Emil Mannerheim defended Finnish independence against Soviet invasion. Finland emerged as a proud Nordic nation maintaining its sovereignty through military strength.
Finnish.playstyle: Defensive and disciplined with strong blockhouses and jaegers. Forest and snowy terrain favor Finnish tactics.
Finnish.bonus: Blockhouses combine barracks and outpost; jaeger training fast

French Canadians.history: Louis-Joseph Papineau championed French-Canadian identity and autonomy within British North America. French Canadian culture persisted and thrived despite English dominance.
French Canadians.playstyle: Voyageur-focused economy with strong coureurs and mounted units. Use forests and waterways effectively.
French Canadians.bonus: Voyageurs gather resources and move fast; fur trade economy

Haitians.history: Toussaint Louverture led the Haitian Revolution, establishing the first Black republic. Haiti's independence shocked the colonial world and inspired liberation movements.
Haitians.playstyle: Revolutionary and defiant with strong maroons and pirates. Irregular warfare and naval attacks; guerrilla tactics effective.
Haitians.bonus: Maroons and pirates strong; irregular warfare effective

Hungarians.history: Lajos Kossuth led the Hungarian Revolution against Habsburg dominance, establishing Hungarian nationalism. Hungary emerged seeking greater autonomy and cultural recognition.
Hungarians.playstyle: Aristocratic and cavalry-focused with strong hussars and grenadiers. Use cavalry charges and well-coordinated assaults.
Hungarians.bonus: Hussar very strong; noble cavalry traditions provide bonuses

Indonesians.history: Diponegoro led Indonesian resistance against Dutch colonialism, becoming a legendary military hero. Indonesia fought for independence and cultural preservation.
Indonesians.playstyle: Jungle-focused with strong irregular units and naval strength. Use terrain advantage and localized uprisings.
Indonesians.units: Native Fighter, War Elephant, Cetbang, Canoe
Indonesians.bonus: Jungle units effective; local uprising capabilities strong

Mayans.history: Jacinto Canek led Mayan resistance during the Caste War, establishing an independent Maya state. Mayans maintained their culture and independence in the Yucatan.
Mayans.playstyle: Native alliance-focused with strong holcan and ceremonial units. Use cenotes and native advantages.
Mayans.units: Holcan, Native Villager, Yucateco Insurgente, Chaac Canoe
Mayans.bonus: Native embassy provides bonuses; holcan javelineers strong

Mexicans (Revolution).history: Benito Juárez led Mexico's liberal reforms and defended against French intervention, establishing secular republic. Mexico emerged with renewed national pride.
Mexicans (Revolution).playstyle: Revolutionary with strong insurgentes and priests. Use rural uprisings and defensive fortifications.
Mexicans (Revolution).bonus: Rural uprising capabilities; priest conversion effective

Napoleonic France.history: Napoleon Bonaparte dominated Europe through military genius and imperial ambition. Napoleonic warfare revolutionized military tactics and reshaped European boundaries.
Napoleonic France.playstyle: Aggressive and dominating with elite cuirassiers and coordinated assaults. Napoleon's strategic brilliance translates to powerful military maneuvers.
Napoleonic France.bonus: Elite cavalry devastating; coordinated assaults extremely powerful

Peruvians.history: Andrés de Santa Cruz united Peru and Bolivia briefly before dissolution, establishing a powerful Andean confederation. Peru emerged as a key South American power.
Peruvians.playstyle: Andean warrior traditions with strong mountain units and cavalry. Effective in high-altitude combat.

Revolutionary France.history: Maximilien Robespierre embodied the French Revolution's radical phase, executing The Terror and attempting to remake French society. Revolutionary fervor spread across Europe.
Revolutionary France.playstyle: Revolutionary and zealous with sans-culottes and popular uprising. Use mass mobilization and passionate infantry.
Revolutionary France.units: Sans Culottes, Voltigeur, Cuirassier, Field Cannon
Revolutionary France.bonus: Revolutionary units strong; popular uprising rapid

Rio Grande.history: Antonio Canales Rosillo led the Rio Grande Republic's brief independence from Mexico. The region's unique culture and strategic location made it a contested frontier.
Rio Grande.playstyle: Frontier raiding with strong cavalry and mercenaries. Use terrain advantages and fast-moving units.
Rio Grande.bonus: Cavalry fast and effective; frontier economy

Romanians.history: Alexandru Ioan Cuza unified Wallachia and Moldavia, establishing modern Romania and pursuing reform. Romania emerged as a Balkan power seeking independence from Ottoman control.
Romanians.playstyle: Balanced military with strong cavalry and dragoons. Effective at defensive positions and mounted assaults.
Romanians.bonus: Dragoon and cavalry effective; outpost units strong

South Africans.history: Paul Kruger led Boer resistance against British colonialism, fighting to preserve Afrikaner independence. South Africa emerged from colonial conflicts seeking self-determination.
South Africans.playstyle: Resilient and adaptive with strong cavalry and settler economy. Use trek wagons and fast-moving mounted units.
South Africans.bonus: Trek wagon generates resources; defensive economy strong

Texians.history: Sam Houston led Texas independence from Mexico and negotiated annexation into the United States. Texas became a frontier power balancing independence and integration.
Texians.playstyle: Frontier militia with strong minutemen and mercenaries. Rapid defense and irregular warfare effective.
Texians.bonus: Militia rapidly rebuilt; frontier defense strong

Yucatan.history: Felipe Carrillo Puerto championed Yucatan independence and indigenous rights. The Yucatan pursued autonomy while maintaining cultural traditions and Maya heritage.
Yucatan.playstyle: Maya-focused with indigenous units and defensive strength. Use cenotes and local uprisings for advantage.
Yucatan.units: Yucateco Insurgente, Cruzob Avenger, Padre, Chaac Canoe
Yucatan.bonus: Indigenous uprising powerful; religious influence strong
